sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system documentation available for this sports game

special_mechanics.name: Jump
special_mechanics.description: Pressing both buttons simultaneously causes the player to jump. Used for defensive positioning and tactical movement.

special_mechanics.name: Dash
special_mechanics.description: Player can dash by pushing the joystick left or right twice. Increases momentum of an offensive throw when combined with throwing action.

special_mechanics.name: Power Shot
special_mechanics.description: Team captain has a specialized offensive throw that travels faster and does more damage than regular throws.

special_mechanics.name: Ball Catch/Intercept
special_mechanics.description: Left button used to catch or intercept opponent's throw when opponent has possession.

special_mechanics.name: Duck
special_mechanics.description: Right button used to duck/evade an attack when opponent has possession.

tier_notes: Tier 3 - Empirical/Qualitative only. Super Dodge Ball is a sports/arcade game (dodgeball) rather than a platformer, so traditional movement physics (walk speed, run speed, gravity, jump physics) are not primary mechanics. Documentation focuses on game modes, regional differences, and team mechanics rather than character physics parameters. Button-based momentum system used for throws rather than continuous physics-based movement.
sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Super Dodge Ball (NES)
